title: No Heroes or villains
wikitext:
[]: No Heroes or villains             Quote Post by GAP » Tue Aug 10, 2010 8:32 pm In most super robot shows, we always have a clear hero who happens to be awesome and a villain who is obviously the bad guy. In Evangelion however, we don't realy have that but instead we only have people doing the best they can with what they had. Even Gendo felt mor elike a tragic figure than some vile human being, Shinji is no Guy shishio or Kamina but he is just Shinji, a troubled young man with many flaws and weaknesses like any other human. I like the fact that they are no clear heores at all as Eva was never a story about heroes being heroic rather it was a coming of age tale for the cahracter Shinji and everyone on the show. Do you guys agree with me on this?

[]: Re: No Heroes or villains             Quote Post by esselfortium » Tue Aug 10, 2010 8:33 pm GAP wrote: In Evangelion however, we don't realy have that but instead we only have people doing the best they can with what they had. Even Gendo felt mor elike a tragic figure than some vile human being,

[]:             Quote Post by soul.assassin » Wed Aug 11, 2010 12:40 am Excluding Keel Lorenz, the ultimate enemy of those characters are themselves.

[]:             Quote Post by esselfortium » Wed Aug 11, 2010 12:44 am And here I was thinking their ultimate enemy was the narrative itself.

[]:             Quote Post by berto » Wed Aug 11, 2010 3:17 am Well, in the classical sence there is no evil characters or villans, simple a lot of conflicting goals. The Angels want Adam and Lillith but if they get it it's the end of humanity, Gendo wants to reunited with his wife, SEELE want to artificially evolve men kind, Misato doesn't like any of that so she sends Shinji to stop them (boy, did that plan ever backfire). Ether way, the reason people and Angels are killing each other is because what they all want conflicts with everyone else's wants or needs. It's rather truthful way of looking at war, actually.

[]: Re: No Heroes or villains             Quote Post by InstrumentalityOne » Wed Aug 11, 2010 5:02 am You should define "Hero" first before jumping to conclusions, but yes, pretty much.

[]:             Quote Post by carla » Wed Aug 11, 2010 8:20 am soul.assassin wrote: The ultimate enemy of those characters are themselves.

[]:             Quote Post by GAP » Wed Aug 11, 2010 9:45 pm the ultimate enemy of those characters are themselves.
